name: German Meatballs
recipeIngredient: 1 pound All-purpose potatoes, scrubbed and sliced, about 3 ½ cups
½ small Head red cabbage, cored and coarsely sliced, about 8 ounces
½ cup Onion, diced, about half a cup
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 4-quart saucepan over medium-high heat, heat 2 tablespoons oil. Add potatoes, cabbage, and onion; cook about 5 minutes until crisp-tender, stirring frequently.
2. Add ½ cup water; cook, covered, for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
3. Meanwhile, in a large bowl, combine veal, pork, bread crumbs, grated lemon peel, 1 tablespoon each: chopped parsley and Worcestershire sauce, salt, and pepper. Using hands or a wooden spoon, blend well.
4. Shape the mixture into 1½-inch balls.
5. In a 12-inch skillet over medium-high heat, heat 1 tablespoon oil. Cook meatballs about 15 minutes, turning frequently until well browned on all sides.
6. Using a slotted spoon, remove meatballs to the saucepan with cabbage mixture; keep warm.
7. Stir flour into drippings in the skillet; add lemon juice, remaining 1 tablespoon parsley, and Worcestershire sauce and capers. Gradually stir in ¾ cup water; increase heat to high. Bring to a boil; reduce heat to low and cook for 1 minute.
8. To serve: Pour mixture over meatball-cabbage mixture in the saucepan; toss well. Spoon into serving bowls. Makes 4 servings.
